message:
fix(redact-memo): char-boundary safe diagnostic prefix slice [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34] (review of 9d87c766)

Self-review of 9d87c766 caught a UTF-8 byte-slicing footgun in the
test diagnostic message:

    &input[..input.len().min(64)]

The fixture set was ASCII-only at landing time so this never fired,
but a future contributor extending the equivalence inputs with a
multi-byte string would hit a byte-boundary panic — exactly the
class of bug MEMORY.md flagged after earlier session 1 fixes via
floor_char_boundary in lib.rs / pages/wizard.rs / ui/app.rs.

Fix:
- Inline safe_prefix(s, max_bytes) helper walks back to the nearest
  is_char_boundary so the assertion message slice is always valid
  UTF-8 regardless of input encoding.
- Add two new fixture inputs that exercise non-ASCII content end-to-
  end: a multi-byte single-line string (🔐 + 测试) and a 16+ KiB blob
  built from "🔐abc".repeat(2048). These pin that:
  1. blake3 over .as_bytes() handles arbitrary byte sequences
     (not just ASCII).
  2. The diagnostic prefix slice doesn't panic (regression gate
     for the original bug).
  3. Memoization invariants hold for non-ASCII content
     (memoized vs uncached output equality).

22/22 src/indexer/redact_secrets.rs lib tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib indexer::redact_secrets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
